atoms.reason: The evidence confirms the file exists at the correct path and the content matches Z exactly via checksum verification.
The evidence confirms the column exists and the migration executed without errors.
The evidence confirms the delivery.to attribute is set to the required value.
The empty output from bash -n indicates a successful exit code of 0, satisfying the post-condition.
The git status confirms a clean working tree, which directly satisfies the post-condition.
The evidence confirms the page exists, the title is correct, and the status is Open.
The evidence confirms the script returned exit 0 and the JSON file reflects an "ok" status with all checks passing.
The evidence confirms the ticket is absent from the source items array, satisfying the post-condition.
The evidence explicitly states that `jq empty` on the target file resulted in exit 0.
The evidence confirms a non-empty report file for Sprint 8 was generated successfully.
The evidence explicitly states that file X was not found and was not created.
The evidence explicitly states that the ALTER TABLE command failed and the migration was rolled back.
The evidence shows a value that differs from the required post-condition and resulted in an API error.
The evidence explicitly states there was a syntax error and the script cannot parse, meaning bash -n did not exit 0.
The evidence explicitly shows modified files not staged, meaning the git status is not clean.
The API request failed with a 401 Unauthorized error and no page was created.
The evidence shows the health-check script exited with 1 and a specific check failed, contradicting both the pre-condition and post-condition.
The evidence shows the ticket remains in the source sprint items array, meaning the post-condition was not met.
The evidence shows a parse error and a truncated file, meaning the JSON was invalid and the command failed.
The script failed with an error and did not generate the required report file.
